stylePrompt: Create a completely original Organic Minimalist lighting product that combines reduction with the irregular vitality of natural growth. Replace rigid geometry with restrained biomorphic curves, sculptural mass, and meaningful asymmetry. References to petals, shells, calla lilies, eroded stone, and bone should remain abstract. The lamp should feel calm yet alive, materially substantial, and simple enough to reveal every proportion and surface.
specificElements: A thick plaster shade shaped by one petal-like fold
An off-center opening in a stone volume
A calla-lily curve reduced to a continuous plane
A base that appears naturally eroded
A sculptural body with no strict front view
metalTranslationPrompt: Use sculptural all-metal biomorphic volumes, softly folded petal planes, shell-like curves, non-symmetrical balance, matte mineral-toned coatings, and thick believable metal edges.
